info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing v2 API
Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Load Balancer

params.name: Name
params.description: The name of the load balancer. Must be unique per region per account, can have a maximum of 32 characters.

params.name: Subnets
params.description: The IDs of the subnets. You can specify only one subnet per Availability Zone.

params.name: SecurityGroups
params.description: The IDs of the security groups for the load balancer

params.name: Scheme
params.description: The scheme for the load balancer. An internet-facing load balancer routes requests from clients to targets over the internet. An internal load balancer routes requests to targets using private IP addresses.

params.name: Type
params.description: The type of load balancer

params.name: IpAddressType
params.description: The type of IP addresses used by the subnets

items.docs: Creates an Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. For more information, see the User Guide for the specific load balancer type.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Load Balancers

params.name: LoadBalancerArns
params.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARN) of the load balancers

params.name: Names
params.description: The names of the load balancers

params.name: Marker
params.description: The marker for the next set of results

params.name: PageSize
params.description: The maximum number of results to return

items.docs: Describes the specified load balancers or all of your load balancers. Returns information about load balancer ARN, DNS name, scheme, VPC, availability zones, type, and state.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Delete a Load Balancer

params.name: LoadBalancerArn
params.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the load balancer

items.docs: Deletes the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Deleting a load balancer also deletes its listeners.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Modify Load Balancer Attributes

params.name: LoadBalancerArn
params.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the load balancer

params.name: Attributes.member.1.Key
params.description: The name of the attribute

params.name: Attributes.member.1.Value
params.description: The value of the attribute

items.docs: Modifies the specified attributes of the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Target Group

params.name: Name
params.description: The name of the target group. Must be unique per region per account.

params.name: Protocol
params.description: The protocol to use for routing traffic to the targets

params.name: Port
params.description: The port on which the targets receive traffic

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The identifier of the VPC for the targets

params.name: HealthCheckProtocol
params.description: The protocol to use for health checks

params.name: HealthCheckPort
params.description: The port to use for health checks

params.name: HealthCheckPath
params.description: The destination for health checks on the targets

params.name: TargetType
params.description: The type of target to register with the target group

items.docs: Creates a target group. Target groups are used to route requests to one or more registered targets. After creating a target group, you can register targets and create listeners or rules to route traffic to the target group.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Target Groups

params.name: LoadBalancerArn
params.description: The ARN of the load balancer

params.name: TargetGroupArns
params.description: The ARNs of the target groups

params.name: Names
params.description: The names of the target groups

params.name: Marker
params.description: The marker for the next set of results

params.name: PageSize
params.description: The maximum number of results to return

items.docs: Describes the specified target groups or all of your target groups. By default, all target groups are described. Alternatively, you can specify target group ARNs or names.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Register Targets with a Target Group

params.name: TargetGroupArn
params.description: The ARN of the target group

params.name: Targets.member.1.Id
params.description: The ID of the target (instance ID, IP address, or Lambda ARN)

params.name: Targets.member.1.Port
params.description: The port on which the target is listening

params.name: Targets.member.1.AvailabilityZone
params.description: The Availability Zone where the target is located

items.docs: Registers the specified targets with the specified target group. If the target is an EC2 instance, it must be in the running state when you register it.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Deregister Targets from a Target Group

params.name: TargetGroupArn
params.description: The ARN of the target group

params.name: Targets.member.1.Id
params.description: The ID of the target

params.name: Targets.member.1.Port
params.description: The port on which the target is listening

items.docs: Deregisters the specified targets from the specified target group. After the targets are deregistered, they no longer receive traffic from the load balancer.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Target Health

params.name: TargetGroupArn
params.description: The ARN of the target group

params.name: Targets.member.1.Id
params.description: The ID of the target

params.name: Targets.member.1.Port
params.description: The port on which the target is listening

items.docs: Describes the health of the specified targets or all of your targets registered with the specified target group.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener

params.name: LoadBalancerArn
params.description: The ARN of the load balancer

params.name: Protocol
params.description: The protocol for connections from clients to the load balancer

params.name: Port
params.description: The port on which the load balancer is listening

params.name: SslPolicy
params.description: The security policy that defines which protocols and ciphers are supported

params.name: DefaultActions.member.1.Type
params.description: The type of action

params.name: DefaultActions.member.1.TargetGroupArn
params.description: The ARN of the target group for forward actions

items.docs: Creates a listener for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer. Listeners check for connection requests from clients using the protocol and port that you configure.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Listeners

params.name: LoadBalancerArn
params.description: The ARN of the load balancer

params.name: ListenerArns
params.description: The ARNs of the listeners

params.name: Marker
params.description: The marker for the next set of results

params.name: PageSize
params.description: The maximum number of results to return

items.docs: Describes the specified listeners or the listeners for the specified Application Load Balancer, Network Load Balancer, or Gateway Load Balancer.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Create a Listener Rule

params.name: ListenerArn
params.description: The ARN of the listener

params.name: Priority
params.description: The rule priority. Must be unique within the listener.

params.name: Conditions.member.1.Field
params.description: The name of the field (host-header, path-pattern, etc.)

params.name: Conditions.member.1.Values
params.description: The condition values

params.name: Actions.member.1.Type
params.description: The type of action

params.name: Actions.member.1.TargetGroupArn
params.description: The ARN of the target group for forward actions

items.docs: Creates a rule for the specified listener. The listener must be associated with an Application Load Balancer. Rules are evaluated in priority order, from the lowest value to the highest value.

info.name: Amazon Elastic Load Balancing Describe Listener Rules

params.name: ListenerArn
params.description: The ARN of the listener

params.name: RuleArns
params.description: The ARNs of the rules

params.name: Marker
params.description: The marker for the next set of results

params.name: PageSize
params.description: The maximum number of results to return

items.docs: Describes the specified rules or the rules for the specified listener. You must specify either a listener or one or more rules.
